reasoning: The question asks if a vaccine will have been developed for Prevotella infection by specific resolution dates in late 2025/early 2026, according to the "List of infectious diseases" Wikipedia page. As of the knowledge cutoff (October 26, 2025) and October 16, 2025, there is no vaccine for Prevotella infection. Prevotella is a genus of bacteria that commonly associates with humans (often a commensal) and sometimes causes opportunistic infections (like periodontitis). Developing a new vaccine is a process that typically takes many years, and there are almost never surprise vaccine approvals for obscure opportunistic pathogens. A literature search only revealed one paper from 2023 discussing the in silico design of a multi-epitope vaccine construct for P. intermedia using immunoinformatics. There are no clinical trials mentioned or active development programs that could lead to a sudden change. Therefore, it is virtually impossible that a prevailing Prevotella vaccine will be developed and added to Wikipedia between October 26, 2025 and January 2026. The probability remains at the minimum possible bound (0.02) for all resolution dates.
